title: Thiollierea pachyphylla
intro: Thiollierea pachyphylla, also known as the 'Queensland Bush Apple', is a species of flowering plant in the family Staphylocactaceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues, including skin infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Thiollierea pachyphylla include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are topical creams and ointments, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine practices.
constituents: I couldn't find any information on the active constituents of Thiollierea pachyphylla.
